posts.title: Firms are built on friction
posts.summary: Coase's 1937 answer to why firms exist, and why cheap agents put pressure on the boundary.

posts.title: A prosthesis over a healthy limb
posts.summary: Ned Beauman describes AI as a crutch that cripples.

posts.title: Quite frankly, I don't remember
posts.summary: Borges on a discussion of immortality at a noisy bar.*

posts.title: A gem backed with foil
posts.summary: The narrative foil comes from jewelers backing gems with foil to make them shine more brightly, and Shakespeare used the image knowingly.

posts.title: It has not escaped our notice
posts.summary: Watson and Crick's quiet sentence about base pairing, copying, and the confidence of leaving the implications open.

posts.title: Rest isn't idleness
posts.summary: Leo Umilio on the approved kind of doing nothing (rest, which serves work) and the suspect kind (idleness, which serves nothing at all).

posts.title: A market for lemons in software

posts.title: A market for lemons
posts.summary: Akerlof on how information asymmetry can drive out quality and distort markets.
